items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians unlikely to be paid out, experts say

children.content: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.

articles.title: Passengers from hantavirus ship transferred to Australian quarantine

children.content: A flight carrying six people who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship lands at an air force base in Perth.

items.title: Video of shark decapitation sparks calls for better protection

children.content: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.

image.altText: hands holding large shark's mouth open.

items.title: How the Kimberley's beasts of burden became a 'menace'

children.content: Before roads and vehicles, teams of donkeys used to haul huge loads across remote parts of Western Australia. But the beloved animal became a declared pest that even the meat trade couldn't save.

image.altText: About 20 donkeys harnessed to a wagon outside a large hotel

items.title: Speeding driver who killed acclaimed novelist jailed for four years

children.content: Lucas Martins Da Silva was driving his hatchback more than 30 kilometres an hour over the speed limit when he struck Brenda Walker in Perth's CBD.

image.altText: A woman stands in front of a body of water smiling.

items.title: Three-month closure for regional and remote courthouses

children.content: Western Australia's top judges
have released a public notice saying many courthouses in regional and remote areas will close for at least three months due to a lack of security.

image.altText: a small brick courthouse with two benches outside

items.title: WA treasurer concedes $217 million cost of racetrack project may blow out

children.content: Despite assurances to the contrary, Rita Saffioti says it may be hard to keep the controversial Burswood racetrack within budget.

gallery.altText: A woman with glasses speaks to reporters

thumbnail.altText: A woman with glasses speaks to reporters

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: The United States and China just made a pact

children.content: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

articles.title: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing

children.content: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.

image.altText: Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump walk in blue suits in front of five American and Chinese flags.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

items.title: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools

children.content: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

image.altText: Jane Caro

items.title: 'Grossly incompetent': Action taken against Latham after online outburst

children.content: Contempt proceedings have been launched against Mark Latham after he attacked a tribunal that found he unlawfully vilified and sexually harassed another state MP.

image.altText: Mark Latham in a black coat and orange time walking

items.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group

children.content: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.

image.altText: Shadowy figures in the foreground on an orange background with an Australian flag and US flag

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a 90s club banger

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

image.altText: A dog is blessed by a priest in Spain on Saint Anthony's Day.

items.title: A Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something much bigger

children.content: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.
A white Chinese research vessel at dock with a city in the background.

items.title: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians

children.content: Seven Palestinians, including a child, have died in Friday's air strikes, which Israel said targeted Hamas militant leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad.

image.altText: A grey multi-level building shows damaged walls and windows as fire burns inside.

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: A little box of joy came into my life at just the right time

children.content: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

gallery.altText: Lapis the bluetit in her nest

thumbnail.altText: Lapis the bluetit in her nest

items.title: WA Premier Roger Cook treads fine line in 'standing up to Canberra'

children.content: Roger Cook welcomes the federal budget's focus on housing, but he's less than thrilled about NDIS changes and the funding of infrastructure and aged care.

image.altText: Roger Cook wears a suit with a serious expression on his face.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: Deaths of Pacific islander farm workers ripple through small country town

children.content: Residents of a WA country town have come together to mourn the deaths of two Pacific island farm workers as investigations into their deaths continue.

image.altText: Two white crosses

items.title: Mouse plague so bad locals say driving feels like 'popping bubble wrap'

children.content: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.

gallery.altText: A close up of a dozen dead mice on a shovel, Robert is wearing blue pants in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a dozen dead mice on a shovel, Robert is wearing blue pants in the background.

items.title: Surfers conquer 'terrifying' tidal rapid in remote WA

children.content: Pro surfers Anthony Walsh and Dylan Graves have conquered the never-before-surfed Kimberley tidal rapid in Western Australia's remote north.

image.altText: A strange wave being surfed.

items.title: Green light for Andrew Forrest's $70m Ningaloo Reef resort

children.content: After almost a decade of planning, mining billionaire Andrew Forrest's plan to build an eco-resort on the shores of the Ningaloo Reef has received the go-ahead.

image.altText: A mountain covered in shrubs and five cabins with balconies lined up next to each other

items.title: Cattle industry loses court appeal over live export ban compensation

children.content: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

gallery.altText: the sun sets over cattle cattle yard with dust rising

thumbnail.altText: the sun sets over cattle cattle yard with dust rising

items.title: Police investigate suspected homicide under 'cover of darkness' in WA farming town

children.content: The man was found unresponsive on a suburban street in Katanning early yesterday morning.

image.altText: police on a street

items.title: Man facing drug charges after woman's body found outside cafe in Joondalup

children.content: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup, with a 35-year-old man charged with a string of drug offences.

image.altText: A note on the cafe window where the woman's body was found.

items.title: Record native title compensation decision hailed as a 'watershed case'

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

articles.title: Record payout after billionaire built mine without traditional owners' permission

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

image.altText: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

gallery.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

thumbnail.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

items.title: How likely is Delta Goodrem to bring home the Eurovision crown?

children.content: In a dazzling show of light and a powerful, pitch-perfect performance, Delta Goodrem may have just given Australia a real shot at winning Eurovision for the first time.

image.altText: Delta Goodrem singing.

articles.title: Delta Goodrem climbs onto her piano and into Eurovision 2026 grand final

children.content: Delta Goodrem will perform in the Eurovision 2026 grand final for Australia after a rousing performance of the song Eclipse during the semifinal.

image.altText: Delta Goodrem standing on a golden piano with jets of fire behind her.

gallery.altText: Delta Goodrem with her arms outstretched standing behind a golden piano.
